Article Title: ‘Ritabrata forged symbol’: AITC files police complaint against rebel leaders
Article Text:
AITC files police complaint against rebel leaders (ANI images)

Seeks immediate legal action

All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) on Sunday lodged a police complaint alleging that several rebel leaders forged the party's name, symbol and official letterhead to impersonate the organisation.In a letter addressed to the Officer-in-Charge of New Town Police Station under the Bidhannagar Police Commissionerate, the AITC sought legal action against Arup Roy, Ritabrata Banerjee, Javed Khan, Sandipan Saha, Biplab Mitra and others over an incident that allegedly took place at Novotel Kolkata Hotel & Residences in New Town on June 22.The party requested the police to treat the complaint as information under Section 173 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023, and initiate action in accordance with the law.The party claimed that the forged letterhead was subsequently used to submit a letter dated June 22, 2026, to the Speaker of the West Bengal Legislative Assembly, with Banerjee allegedly impersonating himself as the "General Secretary" of the AITC."Ritabrata Banerjee forged the Symbol and Name of the AITC to fraudulently create the Letter Head of the AITC using which he submitted a letter dated June 22, 2026 to the Speaker of the West Bengal Legislative Assembly impersonating himself to be the 'General Secretary',” reads the letter.AITC has alleged that the use of its name and election symbol amounted to forgery and impersonation, and has urged the police to investigate the matter and take immediate legal action against those named in the complaint.
Article Summary: All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) files police complaint against rebel leaders. Arup Roy, Ritabrata Banerjee, Javed Khan, Sandipan Saha, Biplab Mitra and others named. AITC alleges that the use of its name and election symbol amounted to forgery and impersonation.

Article Title: 'Resign or join them': Raj Thackeray to top IPS officer over RSS praise
Article Text:
In a Facebook post, Thackeray said a police officer's loyalty should remain solely with the duties and responsibilities of the force

NEW DELHI: MNS chief Raj Thackeray on Saturday criticised senior IPS officer Vishwas Nangare Patil over his speech at a Hindu Sakal Samaj event, saying the officer should resign from the police service and join the RSS or the BJP if he wished to publicly express his admiration for the organisation.In a Facebook post, Thackeray said a police officer's loyalty should remain solely with the duties and responsibilities of the force. He alleged that Nangare Patil appeared eager to publicly display his allegiance to the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS), suggesting the officer had developed "dual loyalty"."Have you (Nangare Patil) forgotten that you are a police officer and that impartiality is one of the foremost expectations from someone in your position? If you have affection or admiration for the RSS, keep it to yourself."If you wish to express it publicly, then resign from service and join the RSS or the BJP. After all, for the past several years, there has practically been a guaranteed rehabilitation scheme for favoured officers. You too would surely be accommodated," Thackeray wrote.The remarks came after an undated video of Nangare Patil addressing a Hindu Sammelan organised by the Sakal Hindu Samaj went viral on Thursday, sparking a political row. The controversy surfaced days after the IPS officer was appointed Nagpur Police Commissioner. The Maharashtra Congress has also sought an inquiry into the speech, alleging it raises questions about his neutrality and compliance with service rules.
Article Summary: MNS chief Raj Thackeray criticised senior IPS officer Vishwas Nangare Patil over his speech at a Hindu Sakal Samaj event. He said a police officer's loyalty should remain solely with the duties and responsibilities of the force. The Maharashtra Congress has sought an inquiry into the speech, alleging it raises questions about his neutrality.

Article Title: 'Betrayal of Hindus by BJP': Uddhav Thackeray on Ram Temple donation 'theft'
Article Text:
Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray on Saturday invoked the alleged theft of donations at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya and accused the BJP of "betraying Hindus of the country."He also vowed that the Shiv Sena (UBT) would lead protests to ensure a "BJP-mukt Ram.""Real Hindutva teaches humanity. We reject your (BJP's) Hindutva of looting temples. We want a 'BJP-mukt Ram,' and Shiv Sena (UBT) will lead such protests," Thackeray said at a rally in the Yavatmal-Washim Lok Sabha constituency.The constituency is represented by Sanjay Deshmukh, one of six MPs who recently left the Shiv Sena (UBT) to join the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, an ally of the BJP.Meanwhile, Uddhav Thackeray also referred to Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut’s claim that the party had not received a receipt for the Rs 1 crore it had donated to the Ram Temple trust."We had given several silver bricks before the construction of the Ram Temple. These silver bricks have been stolen. What happened to the money given by Shiv Sena?" the former Maharashtra chief minister asked.Calling it an "insult" to Hindutva, he added, "This is not only a betrayal of Shiv Sena, but also of Hindus in the country."A former BJP ally, Uddhav Thackeray has been frequently accused by the saffron party of betraying Hindutva following their 2019 split.He later joined hands with the Congress and NCP, long-time political and ideological rivals of his party.An FIR, lodged at the instance of trust member Krishna Mohan, was registered based on the recommendations of a preliminary report by a three-member Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by the BJP-led Uttar Pradesh government after allegations of misappropriation surfaced on June 7.The eight arrested individuals were involved in counting cash and valuables received as donations at the temple. After their arrest, they were remanded to judicial custody till June 29. Investigators have so far recovered Rs 79.85 lakh.
Article Summary: Uddhav Thackeray invoked the alleged theft of donations at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. He accused the BJP of "betraying Hindus of the country" He also vowed that the Shiv Sena (UBT) would lead protests to ensure a "BJP-mukt Ram"

Article Title: Watch: Abhijeet Dipke claims BJP workers assaulted reporters in front of Delhi Police
Article Text:
Abhijeet Dipke shared videos on X alleging that BJP workers assaulted reporters and protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. (Screengrab/X/@abhijeet_dipke)

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke on Friday alleged that BJP workers physically assaulted reporters and protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, sharing videos of the incident on X.In one of the videos, a voice can be heard saying, "our members are getting hit in front of Delhi police ." ChatGPT could not independently verify the claim or the circumstances shown in the footage.Sharing the video, Dipke wrote, "BJP goons physically assaulting reporters and people at Jantar Mantar."In another post, accompanied by a separate video, he wrote, "Authorities have a problem with pillows too now. Wish they were this efficient and proactive on paper leaks."The allegations come as Dipke's sit-in protest at Jantar Mantar demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation entered its seventh day. The protest centres on alleged irregularities in competitive examinations and the NEET paper leak controversy.Earlier in the day, Dipke marked Pradhan's birthday by writing, "Happy Birthday, Pradhan. Please resign," on a whiteboard while supporters sang the birthday song.Addressing the minister in a video, he said, "A very happy birthday to you, Dharmendra Pradhan. Do us a favour and resign. As a birthday gift, we can even send you the resignation letter, you'll only have to put your thumb on it."Dipke has maintained that he will continue the protest until Pradhan resigns. He has also alleged that the Centre has not reached out for talks, claiming, "They are calling us terrorists instead."There was no immediate response from the BJP or Delhi Police to Dipke's allegations.
Article Summary: Abhijeet Dipke shared videos on X alleging that BJP workers assaulted reporters and protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. In one of the videos, a voice can be heard saying, "our members are getting hit in front of Delhi police." ChatGPT could not independently verify the claim or the circumstances shown in the footage.

Article Title: 'Not related to him': Rahul Gandhi expresses regret in defamation case filed by Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi (File Photo)

Poll Do you think alleged defamatory statement by Rahul Gandhi was warranted? Yes, it was justified. No, it was inappropriate.





BHOPAL: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi has approached the Madhya Pradesh high court, expressing regret over his alleged defamatory statement involving union minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan ’s son Kartikeya Singh.The application was filed by Rahul's lawyer on Wednesday, stating that the remark made by the Congress leader was not related to Singh.Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha before a Bhopal court, alleging that Gandhi’s statement had harmed his reputation.In his complaint filed before the court handling cases against MPs and MLAs, Singh alleged that during an election rally in Jhabua in 2018, Gandhi had referred to his name while speaking about the Panama Papers leak scandal.Following the complaint, the court issued a summons directing Gandhi to appear personally. The Congress leader then moved the High Court seeking to quash the summons and the defamation proceedings.Justice Pramod Kumar Agarwal will hear Gandhi’s petition on Thursday.During Wednesday’s hearing, the petitioner produced records of the lower court as directed by the high court. Advocate Sankalp Kochhar appeared for the complainant before the high court.
Article Summary: Kartikeya Singh had filed a defamation complaint against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha. Singh alleged that during an election rally in Jhabua in 2018, Gandhi had referred to his name while speaking about the Panama Papers leak scandal. Gandhi moved the High Court seeking to quash the summons and the defamation proceedings.

Article Title: Banners projecting BJP’s Malhar Patil as “future MP” spark political row in Dharashiv
Article Text: Banners projecting Malhar Patil as a “future MP” have been put up in Dharshiv city, sparking speculation of an internal Mahayuti contest for the 2029 Lok Sabha seat between BJP and Shiv Sena
Article Summary: Banners projecting Malhar Patil as a ‘future MP’ have been put up in Dharshiv city. Speculation of an internal Mahayuti contest for the 2029 Lok Sabha seat between BJP and Shiv Sena.

Article Title: ‘Operation Tiger’ is actually ‘Operation Devendra’ to stop Fadnavis from being a PM contender, claims Uddhav
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray on Saturday launched an attack on six defecting MPs, alleging their exit was part of an “Operation Devendra” aimed at weakening chief minister Devendra Fadnavis ’s prospects in national politics.Addressing a rally in Hingoli, Thackeray said, “This is not Operation Tiger. I suspect it is Operation Devendra. Amit Shah wants to clip Devendra Fadnavis’ wings before he can enter the race for the prime minister’s post. They don’t want any politician from Maharashtra to reach Delhi, so he is being kept occupied here.”He questioned why the MPs joined deputy CM Eknath Shinde ’s Shiv Sena instead of BJP, calling it a “big mystery,” and alleged a larger strategy behind the shift. “If the question arises tomorrow about whom to support for prime minister, these MPs will vote for Amit Shah. This is the real arrangement behind breaking the Shiv Sena,” he said.Taking a swipe at Shinde, Thackeray added, “Note this down. Once his usefulness ends, he will be discarded.”Refuting claims that the defections were for development, Thackeray asked whether their move would stop farmers’ suicides, ensure complete farm loan waiver or secure crop insurance payouts. “Will these people now stand in front of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and raise these questions? They had that courage while they were in Shiv Sena.Now they have become slaves,” he said. He also challenged the defectors to oppose the proposed Shaktipeeth Expressway if they genuinely stood with the people.Apologising to party workers, he said, “I have come here not only to apologise but also to assure you that I will stand shoulder to shoulder with you to fight for your rights and justice. My commitment is to the people.”Shiv Sena (UBT) spokesperson and Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Raut too attacked the defectors. He added, “Eknath Shinde and Narendra Modi are running a factory that manufactures traitors.”
Article Summary: Uddhav Thackeray alleges exit was part of an ‘Operation Devendra’ aimed at weakening chief minister Devendra Fadnavis’s prospects in national politics. He questioned why the MPs joined deputy CM Eknath Shinde ’s Shiv Sena instead of BJP, calling it a ‘big mystery’

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: 16-year-old girl gang-raped by two men aged over 60 near Mumbai
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





THANE: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district, police said on Sunday.The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack, which occurred in a slum colony in Netivali village on Saturday night.According to police, the duo allegedly caught hold of the girl and raped her repeatedly.The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.The accused have been booked under the relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.
Article Summary: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district. The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack. The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.

Article Title: Woman possesses ganja worth over Rs2L, held
Article Text:
A police team immediately reached the spot and at10 pm, and they found the woman riding a scooter on the service road near Tapovan crossing near Adgaon

Nashik





: The Panchavati police on Thursday night arrested a 46-year-old woman who was found carrying ganja worth over Rs 2 lakh for sale in the Panchavati area.“The woman, a resident of Panchavati, already has prior cases under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act registered against her at the Worli police station in Mumbai and the Kasara police station in Thane,” said the police. The police have also booked a man who was about to buy the ganja from the woman.Senior officials of the Panchavati police station said that on Thursday they received information that a woman was about to travel to certain place to sell ganja.Acting on the tip-off, senior PI Gajendra Patil immediately formed a team and dispatched it to the Adgaon area to apprehend the woman.A police team immediately reached the spot and at10 pm, and they found the woman riding a scooter on the service road near Tapovan crossing near Adgaon.The police stopped the woman, checked her scooter and found over 10 kg of ganja worth over Rs 2.1 lakh. The police also seized other articles worth Rs 70,000 she was carrying. Officials said that a case was registered against the concerned woman under relevant sections of the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act.The police later found out that the concerned woman also has cases under the NDPS Act registered against her at the Worli police station in Mumbai in 2018 and Kasara police station in Thane in 2014.
Article Summary: The Panchavati police on Thursday night arrested a 46-year-old woman who was found carrying ganja worth over Rs 2 lakh for sale. The woman, a resident of Panchvati, already has prior cases under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act.

Article Title: Arasakumar arrested in ₹100-crore private school recognition fraud
Article Text:
Arrest

Chennai:





B T Arasakumar, 59, who heads an unregistered association of private schools in Tamil Nadu , was arrested by the central crime branch (CCB) of Chennai police on charges of cheating private school managements across the state of more than ₹100 crore, by promising to secure school recognition, grade upgrades and statutory approvals through his political and official contacts.Originally a DMK functionary, Arasakumar left the party and joined BJP, only to rejoin DMK a few years ago. He was arrested on a complaint by Elangovan, secretary of the Federation of Associations of Private Schools in Tamil Nadu.The complaint said that private school managements across the state had faced difficulties over the past two years in obtaining permanent recognition, grade upgrades and approvals from agencies such as DTCP and CMDA. Arasakumar, who operated an unregistered organisation under the name ‘Tamil Nadu Private Schools Association’, claimed he could obtain these approvals through his political and official connections.Police said he collected money from numerous school administrators but failed to secure the promised approvals or refund the amounts, resulting in a fraud estimated at more than ₹100 crore.Based on the complaint, the CCB registered a case and launched an investigation. Police said he would be produced before a magistrate for remand on Sunday.
Article Summary: T Arasakumar, 59, heads an unregistered association of private schools in Tamil Nadu. He was arrested by the central crime branch (CCB) of Chennai police on charges of cheating private school managements across the state of more than ₹100 crore. He claimed he could obtain these approvals through his political and official connections.

Article Title: Three held for assaulting man, stealing motorcycle in Singaperumal Koil
Article Text:
Busted

Chennai:





Three people, including a 17-year-old boy, were arrested by the Singaperumal Koil police for assaulting a 20-year-old man and stealing his motorcycle on the Chennai-Trichy highway near Chengalpet. Police are searching for their accomplice who sold the stolen motorcycle.The victim, Saravanan , 20, of Padalam near Madhurantakam, was working at a textile shop. On the night of the incident, he was returning home on his motorcycle when three men stopped his bike near Singaperumal Koil on GST Road, assaulted him and rode away the bike.Acting on Saravanan’s complaint, the Singaperumal Koil police reviewed CCTV footage and arrested Rohit, 19, and Rajini, 19, both from Singaperumal Koil, along with the 17-year-old. The juvenile was produced before the Juvenile Justice Board and sent to an observation home, while the two adults were remanded in judicial custody by the Chengalpet Judicial Magistrate Court.Police recovered the stolen motorcycles from two of the accused and are continuing efforts to trace the absconding suspect.
Article Summary: Three people, including a 17-year-old boy, were arrested by the Singaperumal Koil police. Police are searching for their accomplice who sold the stolen motorcycle. The victim, Saravanan, 20, of Padalam near Madhurantakam, was working at a textile shop.

Article Title: Three arrested for gang-raping minor girl in Gerugambakkam
Article Text:
Crimes against children

Chennai:





At least three men have been arrested so far on charges of gang-raping a 17-year-old girl near Gerugambakkam.The girl was travelling home on a two-wheeler with a friend after visiting another friend in the Kundrathur area. The friend, who was drunk, took her to a secluded spot near Gerugambakkam, where he summoned his associates and gangraped her.The girl’s parents filed a complaint with Porur all-women police, who detained a total of six for questioning. After inquiry, police arrested Mohammed Adhvik, 22, from Nandambakkam near Kundrathur. Based on information provided by him, two of his associates — Guruprasad , 20, and Ramesh , 21, both from Gerugambakkam — were also arrested.Investigation revealed the three accused committed the crime while under the influence of intoxicants.
Article Summary: The girl was travelling home on a two-wheeler with a friend after visiting another friend in the Kundrathur area. The friend, who was drunk, took her to a secluded spot near Gerugambakkam where he summoned his associates and gangraped her.

Article Title: Three youths die, another suffers injuries as truck collides with motorcycle near Tirupur
Article Text:
Bala Prakash

Sabari





Times News NetworkCOIMBATORE: Three youths died on the spot and another suffered injuries after a two-wheeler they were riding on collided head-on with a truck at Madathukulam in Tirupur district on Thursday night.The deceased have been identified as Bala Prakash, 28, Sabari , 25, and Dinesh, 26. Their friend, Gunasekaran, 18, sustained severe injuries in the accident. They hailed from the Kadathur Pudur area in Madathukulam taluk, police said.According to reports, the four youths were riding together on a single motorcycle towards Madathukulam, reportedly to change the engine oil of the vehicle.The accident took place on the Kaniyur -Madathukulam Main Road. A truck that was travelling in the opposite direction rammed head-on with their two-wheeler near Kaniyur govt hospital. Prakash, Sabari and Dinesh died on the spot. Police shifted their bodies to the Udumalpet govt hospital for postmortem. Gunasekaran was rushed to a private hospital in Udumalpet and later transferred to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital.The Kaniyur police registered a case and initiated an investigation. Truck driver Sanjay Kumar was detained.
Article Summary: The deceased have been identified as Bala Prakash, 28, Sabari, 25, and Dinesh, 26. Their friend, Gunasekaran, 18, sustained severe injuries in the accident. They hailed from the Kadathur Pudur area in Madathukulam taluk.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Vadakkencherry ward member takes oath of office again
Article Text:
Palakkad:





A ward member of Vadakkencherry grama panchayat on Saturday took the oath of office once again after Kerala high court set aside his earlier oath, taken in the name of former chief minister Oommen Chandy Sunil Chuvattupadam, a Congress member elected from Ward 21 (Panniyankara), took the oath again “in the name of God” before standing committee chairperson Ambili Mohandas.He had earlier taken the oath saying, “By the grace of God, in the name of Oommen Chandy.” Another ward member challenged the validity of the oath before high court. The court declared the oath invalid and directed Chuvattupadam to take a fresh oath within four weeks of receiving the order.The court observed that under Kerala Panchayat Raj Act and Kerala Municipality Act, elected representatives can take oath only “in the name of God” or make a solemn affirmation, and not in the name of any individual.High court had also set aside the oaths taken by 20 BJP councillors of Thiruvananthapuram corporation over similar deviations from the prescribed format.
Article Summary: Sunil Chuvattupadam, a Congress member elected from Ward 21 (Panniyankara), took the oath again ‘in the name of God’ Another ward member challenged the validity of the oath before high court. High court had also set aside the oaths taken by 20 BJP councillors of Thiruvananthapuram corporation.
